title: Human-Authored vs. Synthetic Data
concept: Human-authored instructions (like ShareGPT) capture natural user styles; synthetic instruction datasets (like Alpaca) provide clean volume but can be repetitive.

exercises.prompt: What are we exploring in this lesson?

options.text: How human-written data compares to computer-generated data for training AI.
How to write computer code using HTML and CSS.
How to build a physical computer keyboard from scratch.

exercises.explain: This lesson teaches you the difference between training AI on text written by real humans versus text generated by computer programs.

cards.title: Who Wrote This?
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI learns to chat like a human? It needs to read millions of sentences. But should we feed it real text written by messy, creative humans, or clean, organized text generated by other computers? Let's find out!

cards.title: Human vs. Synthetic Data
cards.body: Human-authored data is created by real people sharing their natural thoughts, typos, and unique ways of speaking. Synthetic data is generated by another computer program, creating large volumes of clean, structured text that can sometimes feel repetitive.

cards.title: Handmade vs. Factory-Made
cards.body: Think of human-authored data like a custom, handmade patchwork quilt. Each patch has a different texture, size, and pattern because different people made them. Synthetic data is like a factory-made blanket: it is perfectly uniform, produced quickly in large amounts, but every section looks almost exactly the same.

cards.title: Spotting the Difference
cards.body: A human-authored instruction might look like: 'hey, can u fix this code real quick? it keeps breaking lol'. A synthetic instruction generated by an AI template might look like: 'Please modify the provided Python function to correct the syntax error.'

cards.title: The Cleanliness Trap
cards.body: Many people think synthetic data is always better because it doesn't have spelling mistakes. In reality, AI trained only on synthetic data can become robotic and struggle to understand real, messy human conversations when people use slang or typos.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare the characteristics of human-authored datasets versus synthetic datasets.

left.text: Messy, realistic, full of slang, shows how people actually chat.

right.text: Clean, highly repetitive, large volume, follows strict templates.

exercises.prompt: What is a major benefit of using human-authored instruction datasets like ShareGPT?

options.text: They capture natural conversation styles and human slang.
They are completely free of spelling mistakes and grammatical errors.
They are generated instantly by computer scripts.
They always follow the exact same template.

exercises.explain: Human-authored datasets, like ShareGPT, contain real conversations, which naturally include typos, slang, and diverse formatting that help AI learn how humans actually talk.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Synthetic datasets make it easier to generate huge volumes of instruction data quickly.
exercises.explain: Synthetic datasets can be generated in massive quantities very quickly because computers, not humans, are writing them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about computer-generated instruction sets.
exercises.sentence: Data generated by another AI program is called ________ data.
exercises.explain: Synthetic data is generated by algorithms or AI models, making it easy to create in large amounts, though it can lack the natural variety of human writing.

exercises.prompt: Which of these instructions is most likely synthetic (generated by an AI template)?

options.text: Option A: "quick question, why is my code looping forever? helper plz"
Option B: "Identify the logical error in the following code block and suggest a fix."
Option C: "so i tried to run this and got a weird error message..."

exercises.explain: Option B is synthetic because it follows a highly structured, formal, and repetitive template typical of computer-generated data. Option A is human-authored.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a new AI assistant. Why would you want to use a mix of both human-authored instructions and synthetic data?

options.text: Human data is generated by code, while synthetic data is written by students.
Synthetic data has more typos and slang than human data.
Human data captures natural conversation styles, while synthetic data provides clean, high-volume templates.
Synthetic data is always worse and should never be used to train AI models.

exercises.explain: Human-authored data provides the natural variety and quirks of real communication, while synthetic data offers clean, high-volume training examples that might lack realistic diversity. Combining both gives AI models the best of both worlds!
